title: Decaneuropsis craibiana
taxonomy: Decaneuropsis craibiana is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in the tropical regions of Thailand and Myanmar. The classification of Decaneuropsis craibiana places it within the kingdom Plantae, class Magnoliopsida, order Magnoliales, and family Annonaceae. At the genus and species levels, its classification is within the genus Decaneuropsis and species D. craibiana. The Annonaceae family includes over 2,300 species of flowering plants, many of which are tropical and produce edible fruits.
uses: Decaneuropsis craibiana has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, although limited research exists on this topic. Its extracts and compounds have been found to possess antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Some studies suggest that Decaneuropsis craibiana may have anti-inflammatory properties, which could be beneficial in treating various health conditions.
